refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: supporting achievement of the cycle time target for the specified cycle. It is already expressed in the problem domain (what) without specifying how to achieve it, thus it is solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and verify that the 95th percentile (p95) cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load handling mechanism speed and efficiency
Optimize dump mechanism speed and timing
Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time
Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time conditions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time

potential_sources: Interdependence between load, travel, and dump operations timing
coupling_check.notes: Selecting coordination as the DP explicitly addresses the integration of sub-operations to meet cycle time targets, reducing coupling by managing dependencies at the operational level.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate measurement and control of sub-operation timings
Risk that external site conditions may vary beyond modeled parameters affecting cycle time
Assumes operational policies allow for dynamic coordination adjustments
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including real-time monitoring and adaptive control in the DP to handle variability in site conditions and operational policies.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Coordinates load, travel, and dump operations across vehicles and teleoperation to minimize idle time

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchange status and position data with vehicles

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Send coordinated operation commands to vehicles and teleoperation

module_containment_scores.notes: Central coordination logic fits well here with minimal diffusion outside.
Vehicles execute commands but do not coordinate overall operations; would cause diffusion of coordination logic.
Teleoperation handles remote control but not overall coordination; poor containment.
Safety is orthogonal to coordination; containment very weak.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the coordination DP because it is designed to manage and optimize operations across vehicles and teleoperation, minimizing idle time effectively within a single cohesive module.
containment_risks: Potential complexity growth in NGHE Solution as it handles coordination logic and interfaces with multiple modules.
Dependency on timely and reliable telemetry and network link interfaces may affect coordination effectiveness.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on coordinating load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time, which directly supports achieving the customer-defined cycle time target.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as coordination logic is centralized within the NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (1), indicating effective encapsulation of the coordination DP. Other modules have poor containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring the cycle time p95 against the CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, as well as ensuring compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear, as it is responsible for coordinating operations across vehicles and teleoperation to meet cycle time targets.

recommended_revisions: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module to manage potential scalability issues.
Ensure robust and reliable telemetry and network link interfaces to support effective coordination.
Consider defining clear interface contracts and error handling strategies for external interfaces to mitigate dependency risks.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and minimal diffusion risks. The NGHE Solution module effectively encapsulates the coordination DP with high containment and low diffusion scores, and verification feasibility is established. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary complexity by stopping decomposition here and focusing on monitoring and managing identified risks.

revise.why: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module and ensure robust telemetry and network interfaces to maintain safety and scalability.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can effectively coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to meet cycle time targets.
Verification methods for cycle time and safety compliance are reliable and feasible.
open_risks: Potential scalability issues due to complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module.
Dependency risks from telemetry and network link interfaces.
evidence_needed: Performance data confirming cycle time p95 meets CT_target under operational conditions.
Verification reports on compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
Monitoring data on module complexity and interface reliability.
